input.headline: The receipt myth and why you shouldn't buy a warranty | Money newsletter

raw.headline: The receipt myth and why you shouldn't buy a warranty | Money newsletter

winner: I paid extra for the 'no-issues' warranty on my blender—turns out it was just a $20 IOU from 1998 with someone named Gary's Garbage. The receipt I saved? Just a napkin with 'You got scammed' written in ketchup.

candidates.text: I bought a 10-year warranty on my coffee maker—now I’m getting a "refund" every time I make a cup: *$0.50 per splash*.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist on warranty refunds, specific and concise with a clever play on 'splash'"}

candidates.text: I tried to return my toaster with a warranty, but the store clerk said I needed a receipt — turns out, the warranty was just a bread crumb trail.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "The joke is concise, surprising, and specific with a clever play on 'bread crumb trail' related to both the toaster and warranty."}

candidates.text: I bought a warranty for my toaster just in case it forgot how to make toast — turns out, the receipt was its last hope for redemption.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist with a specific scenario, concise and naturally funny"}

candidates.text: I bought a warranty just in case my toaster turned into a time machine — turns out, the receipt was the real magic trick all along.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist with a specific, relatable scenario; concise and naturally funny."}

candidates.text: I paid extra for the 'no-issues' warranty on my blender—turns out it was just a $20 IOU from 1998 with someone named Gary's Garbage. The receipt I saved? Just a napkin with 'You got scammed' written in ketchup.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist with specific details (IOU from Gary's Garbage, ketchup on a napkin) adds humor and ties well to the topic."}

candidates.text: I finally realized that a product warranty is just a high-priced insurance policy for the guilt of realizing I should have just kept the receipt.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist on warranties and receipts, concise and specific with a touch of dark humor."}

candidates.text: I tried to return my broken toaster without a receipt, but the cashier told me the store has no record of the sale, so I had to stay behind and explain the entire emotional history of our relationship to the toaster until he agreed to take it back.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Creative and specific scenario with a surprising twist and emotional exaggeration for humor."}

candidates.text: Why did the receipt get a complex? It thought it needed a warranty for all its *changes*.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat clever with the wordplay on 'changes' and 'warranty,' but it's a bit forced and lacks specificity in connecting to the receipt myth or warranties. It’s not offensive, but it falls short of being truly surprising or concise."}

candidates.text: I tried to buy a warranty for my receipt, but it was a waste of paper.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat clever with the play on 'warranty for a receipt,' but it lacks full specificity and the punchline feels slightly forced. It's not offensive or generic, but the humor could be sharper."}
